title: MemberScopes
description: The fine-grained, per-module access grants a viewing member holds over a subject member's data (FR-HH-001). Provided by Module 14 and ENFORCED by every module on every cross-user request. This contract describes WHAT was granted; it is NOT itself the enforcement point. Enforcement is server-side: the acting identity is derived from the validated session, the MemberScope grant is looked up server-side, and a denied access is audited (SC-015). UI-layer filtering alone is non-compliant (FR-HH-001). Default for any (viewer, subject, module) tuple with no explicit grant is NO ACCESS (least privilege, Constitution V). This contract carries NO financial values and NO secrets.

subject_profile_id.description: The member whose data is being shared (the data owner). Each subject controls the grants over their own data (a member grants visibility into their finances; it is not imposed on them, except guardian visibility over minors which the household owner/admin configures).

grants.description: Per (viewer, module) grants over this subject. Absence of a grant = no access (least privilege). MUST NOT be relied on by the client for enforcement.

viewer_profile_id.description: The member granted visibility into the subject's data for this module.

module_id.description: The bounded-context module the grant applies to. Grants are per-module so a member can be given, e.g., read access to Bills but not Rewards (least privilege, FR-HH-001).

access_level.description: none = explicit deny (overrides any inherited default); read = may view the subject's data for this module; propose = may view AND surface a recommend-only proposal the subject must still execute (FinOS never moves money — FR-X-003). There is intentionally NO 'write'/'execute' level: no member can move another's money.

expires_at.description: Optional time-boxed grant; past this instant the grant is treated as none (revoked member sees the Empty state immediately — ux-foundations §5.5).

freshness.description: Freshness of the scope snapshot. A revocation MUST take effect immediately server-side regardless of this snapshot; consumers MUST NOT serve cached scope grants once a revocation event is observed (ux-foundations §5.5 — revoked member sees Empty state with no cached data).
